tweets.title: British MoD Minister Luke Pollard at #GASCC2026 today: ✅ New Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) named “Storm Fighter,” backed by £300m ($405m) in funding ✅ Will act as “guardian angel and attack dog” alongside RAF’s crewed fleet (Typhoon, F-35B/A, future Tempest/GCAP) ✅ Pollard: Storm Fighter will make the RAF “Europe’s first 6th generation Air Force” ✅ Sits within £5bn UK investment in drones/uncrewed systems under the Defence Investment Plan Conceptually echoes Project Mosquito (2021, cancelled 2022) Frankly I would like to flag execution risk but hope it proves me wrong (for the sake of better tech aviation).
tweets.content:
British MoD Minister Luke Pollard at #GASCC2026 today:
✅ New Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) named “Storm Fighter,” backed by £300m ($405m) in funding
✅ Will act as “guardian angel and attack dog” alongside RAF’s crewed fleet (Typhoon, F-35B/A, future Tempest/GCAP)
✅ Pollard: Storm Fighter will make the RAF “Europe’s first 6th generation Air Force”
✅ Sits within £5bn UK investment in drones/uncrewed systems under the Defence Investment Plan

Conceptually echoes Project Mosquito (2021, cancelled 2022)

Frankly I would like to flag execution risk but hope it proves me wrong (for the sake of better tech aviation).
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: For us (non participants) things were looking good in Middle East for a while but looks like it won’t die down anytime soon. Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari warned: ✅ Strait of Hormuz interference by US = red line, non-negotiable ✅ If Trump strikes Iran’s infrastructure as threatened, IRGC will target “all infrastructure” still standing across the Gulf region ✅ Comes after 5th consecutive night of US strikes + reimposed naval blockade on Iranian ports ✅ Trump had threatened to hit Iranian power plants and bridges unless Tehran returns to talks Escalation trajectory now bypassing the June MOU framework entirely.
tweets.content:
For us (non participants) things were looking good in Middle East for a while but looks like it won’t die down anytime soon.

Iranian military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari warned:
✅ Strait of Hormuz interference by US = red line, non-negotiable
✅ If Trump strikes Iran’s infrastructure as threatened, IRGC will target “all infrastructure” still standing across the Gulf region
✅ Comes after 5th consecutive night of US strikes + reimposed naval blockade on Iranian ports
✅ Trump had threatened to hit Iranian power plants and bridges unless Tehran returns to talks

Escalation trajectory now bypassing the June MOU framework entirely.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: 🚨 PAF Launches Heavy Air Strikes on Alleged Rebel Training Camp in Bannu, Pakistan Footage from earlier today shows the aftermath of multiple Pakistan Air Force (PAF) airstrikes targeting a building belonging to an alleged educational institute in the Narmikhel area of Bakakhel, Bannu district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan. According to Pakistani sources, the facility was allegedly being used as a training camp by armed rebels. The full extent of the damage and any casualties remains unknown.
tweets.content:
🚨 PAF Launches Heavy Air Strikes on Alleged Rebel Training Camp in Bannu, Pakistan

Footage from earlier today shows the aftermath of multiple Pakistan Air Force (PAF) airstrikes targeting a building belonging to an alleged educational institute in the Narmikhel area of Bakakhel, Bannu district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

According to Pakistani sources, the facility was allegedly being used as a training camp by armed rebels.

The full extent of the damage and any casualties remains unknown.

tweets.title: 🚨 Deadly Ambush Rocks Mastung, more than 7 Pakistani Security Personnel Reportedly Killed Reportedly, armed rebels carried out a coordinated ambush on a convoy of Pakistani security forces in the Khadkocha area of Mastung district, Balochistan, leaving more than seven security personnel dead after an intense exchange of fire. According to local sources, fierce clashes followed the attack. Large reinforcements of Pakistani troops were seen moving toward the ambush site, while traffic on the Quetta to Karachi Highway was completely suspended as security forces launched a response operation.
tweets.content:
🚨 Deadly Ambush Rocks Mastung, more than 7 Pakistani Security Personnel Reportedly Killed

Reportedly, armed rebels carried out a coordinated ambush on a convoy of Pakistani security forces in the Khadkocha area of Mastung district, Balochistan, leaving more than seven security personnel dead after an intense exchange of fire.

According to local sources, fierce clashes followed the attack. Large reinforcements of Pakistani troops were seen moving toward the ambush site, while traffic on the Quetta to Karachi Highway was completely suspended as security forces launched a response operation.


Video
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: Many of you asked about the Saudi strike on Sana'a Airport (Jul 13). Here is what we know so far ✅ Weapon used: Storm Shadow cruise missile, confirmed by Yemeni military and government sources, reported by many outlets ✅ Goal: stop an Iranian plane (carrying a Houthi group) from landing ✅ Result: the runway was not fully destroyed (usually they aren’t - idea is always to make them non functional) and plane landed at Hodeidah instead ✅ Saudi Arabia has not confirmed the strike; reports say it is blaming the Aden-based Yemeni government ✅ Claims of ~10 missiles fired by Saudi Eurofighters from long range come from only one source not confirmed. Will keep tracking this and share updates as more details come out
tweets.content:
Many of you asked about the Saudi strike on Sana'a Airport (Jul 13). Here is what we know so far

✅ Weapon used: Storm Shadow cruise missile, confirmed by Yemeni military and government sources, reported by many outlets
✅ Goal: stop an Iranian plane (carrying a Houthi group) from landing
✅ Result: the runway was not fully destroyed (usually they aren’t - idea is always to make them non functional) and plane landed at Hodeidah instead
✅ Saudi Arabia has not confirmed the strike; reports say it is blaming the Aden-based Yemeni government
✅ Claims of ~10 missiles fired by Saudi Eurofighters from long range come from only one source not confirmed.

Will keep tracking this and share updates as more details come out
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: Not worried about Carlyle's stake in Micropack. India has credible domestic PCB alternatives (Epitome, Fine-Line, Ascent, Sulakshana) if supply needs to shift. The real question is access: ✅ Does this open a door into other sensitive programs Micropack touches ISRO, DRDO, missile guidance PCBs ✅ What IP/data visibility does a US-controlling stake get on classified-adjacent work ✅ Any FDI/security clearance conditions attached to the deal
tweets.content:
Not worried about Carlyle's stake in Micropack. India has credible domestic PCB alternatives (Epitome, Fine-Line, Ascent, Sulakshana) if supply needs to shift.
The real question is access:
✅ Does this open a door into other sensitive programs Micropack touches ISRO, DRDO, missile guidance PCBs
✅ What IP/data visibility does a US-controlling stake get on classified-adjacent work
✅ Any FDI/security clearance conditions attached to the deal

tweets.title: In recent times, several mid and senior level scientists have left ISRO, and the response now appears to be tighter restrictions on those seeking to resign. Retention cannot be built on restrictions. If you want to keep talented scientists, give them meaningful opportunities, greater autonomy, challenging programmes, and clear career prospects. Otherwise, let them move on. The same principle applies across our strategic R&D ecosystem DRDO, HAL, NAL, and others. India has an exceptional pool of scientific talent, but too often its potential is constrained by mediocre decision-making. Technology is sovereignty. The sooner we recognise that, the better.
tweets.content:
In recent times, several mid and senior level scientists have left ISRO, and the response now appears to be tighter restrictions on those seeking to resign.

Retention cannot be built on restrictions. If you want to keep talented scientists, give them meaningful opportunities, greater autonomy, challenging programmes, and clear career prospects. Otherwise, let them move on.

The same principle applies across our strategic R&D ecosystem DRDO, HAL, NAL, and others.

India has an exceptional pool of scientific talent, but too often its potential is constrained by mediocre decision-making.

Technology is sovereignty. The sooner we recognise that, the better.

tweets.title: Ball is in your court now @ndtv !
tweets.content:
Ball is in your court now @ndtv !

ANI (@ANI)

With reference to reports circulating in the media regarding the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project (KKNPP) Units 3 & 4, it is clarified that the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) contract for the Common Services – Balance of Plant (BoP) package was awarded to Reliance Infrastructure Ltd. in 2018 through a Public Tender process. The scope of the contract includes Engineering, Procurement/Supply, Construction and Commissioning of Common service facilities. These facilities are of conventional nature and are typically found in thermal power plants as well as other process industries. They are not related to nuclear safety or nuclear security systems. NPCIL reiterates that the information claimed to be available in the public domain pertains only to conventional Balance of Plant (BoP) common service facilities and does not relate to any nuclear safety - or nuclear security-related systems or information: NPCIL

— https://nitter.net/ANI/status/2077609852004946084#m

tweets.title: Some of you have been asking where CATS Warrior stands in the development cycle, so here’s the comparison: ✅ Ghatak: DAC granted AoN on 27 March 2026 under official designation RPSA (Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft), for 4 squadrons (~60-80 airframes) ✅ CATS Warrior: technology demonstrator built, no AoN yet Remember funding in India’s procurement system usually begins at DAC.
tweets.content:
Some of you have been asking where CATS Warrior stands in the development cycle, so here’s the comparison:

✅ Ghatak: DAC granted AoN on 27 March 2026 under official designation RPSA (Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft), for 4 squadrons (~60-80 airframes)
✅ CATS Warrior: technology demonstrator built, no AoN yet

Remember funding in India’s procurement system usually begins at DAC.

tweets.title: Great initiative 🔥
tweets.content:
Great initiative 🔥

Amar Govindarajan (@amargov)

want to gently circulate indiabuild.wiki - inspired by bomwiki.com

it's a kind of tech map and sovereignty roadmap.

1. idea is to build a catalog of 10,000 products/foundational capabilities India must build. Could range from EDA software to polysilica to naval propulsion systems to port cranes.

2. Will map out current builders, where they're stuck and also add a policy layer on top of this (what is needed to make something happen for ex.).

3. Will also have crowd sourced (including from builders, academics, enthusiasts) inputs leading to our AI engine fleshing out the details.

4. one idea is - once you map out the tech tree - could we identify adjacent players for certain products? for ex: can Bull (CBE based backhoe brand) make port cranes if given support?

4. everything is a little fragile and slightly in bad aesthetic now - it's a side (but important) project largely made this past weekend.

5. it will take a month or so to properly stabilise and have more meaningful data but in the meanwhile I hope you get a sense of what we're trying to build.

6. companies/start-ups and individual builders will also be able to plug in and push their needs, views and accomplishments through an agent very soon.

you can see indiabuild.wiki

— https://nitter.net/amargov/status/2077444897599373800#m

tweets.title: Dear Mr @MirwaizKashmir, Words matter. It's not the "other side of the LoC." It's "Pakistan Occupied Jammu & Kashmir". Facts don't change with terminology.
tweets.content:
Dear Mr @MirwaizKashmir,

Words matter.

It's not the "other side of the LoC." It's "Pakistan Occupied Jammu & Kashmir".

Facts don't change with terminology.

Mirwaiz Umar Farooq (@MirwaizKashmir)

Deeply saddened and pained by reports of loss of life of civilians and police personnel in the continuing unrest on the other side of LOC especially in Rawalakot and Poonch. My thoughts and prayers are with the bereaved families and all those affected by these tragic events.

I am also concerned by the ongoing discussions regarding representation and the constitutional and political status of the large number of Kashmiri State Subjects residing in Pakistan. Questions touching upon identity, representation and political rights require sensitivity, dialogue and broad public confidence.

The legitimacy and strength of any system of governance rests ultimately upon the consent, trust and confidence of the people it serves.Our history and our shared social,cultural and family ties across the Line of Control remind us that happenings in one part of Jammu and Kashmir are deeply felt in another, and among our diaspora communities
around the world.

My family has a longstanding historical associations with the social, educational, religious and political development of communities across Jammu and Kashmir, including those on the other side of LOC .

As I continue to hear expressions of anguish and distress from people across the LOC reaching out regarding the unrest, I urge the Govt of Pakistan and the local Government there, to prioritise dialogue, restraint and engagement, resolving the differences through consultation and accommodation rather than confrontation, with respect for human rights and the sanctity of human life remaining paramount.

I also appeal to protesting parties to exercise wisdom and responsibility. Both need to work towards restoring peace and public confidence at the earliest.

I pray for lasting peace and stability for all people of Jammu and Kashmir on both sides of the LOC and its resolution.

— https://nitter.net/MirwaizKashmir/status/2077295043900019025#m

tweets.title: Su-30MKI’s radar upgrade isn’t just the AESA (Virupaksha) The radome is being redesigned too, per SoW documents. ✅ New tapered/octagonal AESA array face: 2,400 radiating elements (48×32 core + graduated taper rows) ✅ Companion radome redesign underway, aimed at improving RF transparency of the nose section ✅ Mold-line drawings show a slender ogive profile 7.5° half-angle, ~2.6m slant length Once achieved this will be the biggest AESA on any aircraft (if we go by public figures J20 was biggest so far with 2200)
tweets.content:
Su-30MKI’s radar upgrade isn’t just the AESA (Virupaksha)
The radome is being redesigned too, per SoW documents.

✅ New tapered/octagonal AESA array face: 2,400 radiating elements (48×32 core + graduated taper rows)
✅ Companion radome redesign underway, aimed at improving RF transparency of the nose section
✅ Mold-line drawings show a slender ogive profile 7.5° half-angle, ~2.6m slant length

Once achieved this will be the biggest AESA on any aircraft (if we go by public figures J20 was biggest so far with 2200)

tweets.title: 🚨 BLA Names Its Target as Lashkar-E-Taiba/ISIS : A Three-Way Clash on the Horizon? The regional proxy war just hit a breaking point. The BLA has officially named Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) as a direct collaborator with Shafiq Mengal - ISIS, Pak army and former Afghan National Army. By explicitly naming LeT, the BLA is drawing new battle lines. With the TTP already hostile toward both ISIS and LeT, we are watching a potential convergence of insurgent fronts against a complex web of state and non-state actors. As these factions align, the question isn’t if a collision is coming, it’s who pulls the trigger first. Recently BLA targetted Shafiq mengal at is residence in Khuzdar Balochistan
tweets.content:
🚨 BLA Names Its Target as Lashkar-E-Taiba/ISIS : A Three-Way Clash on the Horizon?

The regional proxy war just hit a breaking point. The BLA has officially named Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) as a direct collaborator with Shafiq Mengal - ISIS, Pak army and former Afghan National Army.

By explicitly naming LeT, the BLA is drawing new battle lines. With the TTP already hostile toward both ISIS and LeT, we are watching a potential convergence of insurgent fronts against a complex web of state and non-state actors.
As these factions align, the question isn’t if a collision is coming, it’s who pulls the trigger first.

Recently BLA targetted Shafiq mengal at is residence in Khuzdar Balochistan
tweets.author: @OsintTV
